title: Convolvulus papiru
taxonomy: Convolvulus papiru belongs to the family Convolvulaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as morning glories or bindweeds. The genus Convolvulus is a part of this family and comprises around 250 species of annual or perennial herbs, shrubs, or vines. These plants are native to various parts of the world, including tropical and subtropical regions, and are characterized by their distinctive, trumpet-shaped flowers. The family Convolvulaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, including Ipomoeoideae and Convolvuloideae. Within the genus Convolvulus, the classification is often based on the morphology and distribution of the species.

intro: Convolvulus papiru, also known as Egyptian convolvulus or morning glory, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region and Africa, belonging to the Convolvulaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Convolvulus papiru include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Convolvulus papiru is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and skin disorders.
